storyRequirements: - Keep the story age-appropriate for 3rd graders (ages 8-9)
- Use fun, relatable scenarios (animals, food, toys, school, etc.)
- Make the math problem clear and solvable
- End with a clear question
- Provide the answer on a new line in the format "Answer: [your answer]"
- Keep the story to one paragraph
- Use only topics and concepts appropriate for 3rd grade math

topics.name: Multiplication
topics.description: Understanding multiplication through skip counting, arrays, and equal groups
objectives: Understand multiplication as repeated addition
Use skip counting to solve multiplication problems
Represent multiplication using arrays and groups
Solve word problems involving equal groups
Apply the commutative property of multiplication
Connect multiplication and division as inverse operations

ai.guidelines: Focus on repeated addition, groups, arrays, and skip counting (2-12 times tables)

topics.name: Division
topics.description: Understanding division through equal sharing, grouping, and fact families
objectives: Understand division as equal sharing and grouping
Solve division problems using various strategies
Connect division to multiplication through fact families
Solve word problems involving division
Understand remainders in division
Use arrays to model division problems

ai.guidelines: Focus on equal sharing, grouping, and the relationship with multiplication

topics.name: Fractions
topics.description: Understanding fractions as parts of a whole, equivalent fractions, and comparison
objectives: Understand fractions as parts of a whole
Identify equivalent fractions
Compare fractions with same denominators or numerators
Add and subtract simple fractions
Simplify fractions to lowest terms

ai.guidelines: Focus on parts of a whole, equivalent fractions, comparing fractions, and simple addition/subtraction

topics.name: Measurement & Data
topics.description: Understanding area, perimeter, and volume through hands-on measurement
objectives: Find area by counting unit squares
Calculate area using length × width
Find perimeter by adding all sides
Understand volume as space inside 3D objects
Solve real-world measurement problems

ai.guidelines: Focus on area (length x width), perimeter (adding all sides), and volume (counting cubes or length x width x height)

storyRequirements: - Keep the story age-appropriate for 4th graders (ages 9-10)
- Use engaging, realistic scenarios that connect to their experiences
- Make the math problem clear and solvable using 4th grade methods
- End with a clear question
- Provide the answer on a new line in the format "Answer: [your answer]"
- Keep the story to one paragraph
- Use only topics and concepts appropriate for 4th grade math (Common Core 4th grade standards)

topics.name: Operations & Algebraic Thinking
topics.description: Multiplicative comparisons, prime and composite numbers, factors and multiples
objectives: Solve multiplicative comparison word problems
Identify prime and composite numbers
Find factors and multiples of whole numbers
Recognize and extend number patterns
Use mathematical reasoning and problem-solving strategies

ai.guidelines: Focus on multiplicative comparisons ("3 times as many"), prime/composite numbers, factors and multiples, number patterns, multi-step word problems

topics.name: Base Ten
topics.description: Place value, rounding, and multi-digit operations
objectives: Understand place value to 1,000,000
Compare and order multi-digit numbers
Round numbers to any place value
Add and subtract multi-digit numbers
Multiply and divide multi-digit numbers
Use number patterns and properties
Understand decimal place value for tenths, hundredths, and thousandths

ai.guidelines: Focus on place value to 1,000,000, rounding to any place, multi-digit addition/subtraction/multiplication, division with remainders, understanding place value relationships, and decimal place value (identifying digits in tenths/hundredths/thousandths places, understanding the value of decimal digits, writing decimals in expanded form, the 10x relationship between adjacent decimal places). Note: decimal place value is about understanding positions/values, not about converting fractions to decimals or decimal arithmetic (those belong to Fractions 4th)

topics.name: Fractions 4th
topics.description: Equivalent fractions, comparison, addition, subtraction, and decimal notation
objectives: Understand equivalent fractions
Compare and order fractions
Add and subtract fractions with like denominators
Multiply fractions by whole numbers
Express fractions with denominators of 10 as decimals
Compare decimal notations for fractions
Add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals
Solve word problems involving fractions
Locate and mark decimals on a number line

ai.guidelines: Focus on equivalent fractions, comparing fractions with different denominators, adding/subtracting fractions with like denominators, multiplying fractions by whole numbers, converting between mixed numbers and improper fractions, decimal notation for fractions (including placing decimals on a number line), decimal operations (adding, subtracting, multiplying, and dividing decimals)

topics.name: Measurement & Data 4th
topics.description: Unit conversions, area, perimeter, angles, and data representation
objectives: Convert between different units of measurement
Solve problems involving measurement and conversion
Read and interpret analog clocks
Find area and perimeter of rectangles
Understand angle measurement and classification
Create and interpret line plots
Solve problems using measurement data
Apply measurement concepts to real-world problems

ai.guidelines: Focus on unit conversions (metric and customary), area and perimeter formulas, line plots with fractions, angles and angle measurement, solving problems involving measurement

topics.name: Geometry
topics.description: Points, lines, angles, and classification of shapes
objectives: Identify and draw points, lines, line segments, rays, and angles
Classify triangles by their sides and angles
Classify quadrilaterals by their properties
Identify lines of symmetry in shapes
Recognize parallel and perpendicular lines
Measure and classify angles (acute, right, obtuse, straight)
Find a missing side length given the area or perimeter of a square or rectangle
Find the area and perimeter of composite shapes built from squares and rectangles
Find the original area of a rectangle after it is transformed into a square
Find square photo side lengths in a rectangular collage using total area and a side-length ratio

ai.guidelines: Focus on points/lines/rays/angles, classifying triangles and quadrilaterals by their properties, line symmetry, parallel and perpendicular lines

topics.name: Binary Operations
topics.description: Understanding binary numbers and performing operations in base-2
objectives: Understand what binary numbers are
Convert between binary and decimal numbers
Understand place value in binary (powers of 2)
Add binary numbers using the binary addition rules
Subtract binary numbers using borrowing in binary
Multiply binary numbers using shift and add
Divide binary numbers to find quotients
Compare binary numbers by value
Recognize patterns in binary counting

ai.guidelines: Focus on converting between binary and decimal, adding binary numbers, subtracting binary numbers (no negative results), multiplying small binary numbers, and dividing binary numbers (clean division, no remainders). Keep all numbers in the 1-15 decimal range.

topics.name: Algebra
topics.description: Pre-algebra foundations: variables, evaluating expressions, one-step equations, and patterns
objectives: Understand that a letter can stand for an unknown number
Identify the variable in an expression or equation
Evaluate simple expressions for a given whole-number value
Solve one-step addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division equations
Apply a rule to find the output (or input) of a number machine
Extend growing number patterns and describe their rules

ai.guidelines: Focus on using a letter as a variable for an unknown number, evaluating simple expressions for a given whole-number value (like 3 × n + 2 when n = 4), solving one-step addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division equations, applying input-output rules, and extending growing number patterns. Keep numbers within 0-100 and all answers whole numbers.

storyRequirements: - Keep the story age-appropriate for 5th graders (ages 10-11)
- Use engaging, realistic scenarios that connect to their experiences
- Make the math problem clear and solvable using 5th grade methods
- End with a clear question
- Provide the answer on a new line in the format "Answer: [your answer]"
- Keep the story to one paragraph
- Use only topics and concepts appropriate for 5th grade math (Common Core 5th grade standards)

topics.name: Operations & Algebraic Thinking 5th
topics.description: Numerical expressions with parentheses, prime factorization, and paired number patterns
objectives: Evaluate numerical expressions containing parentheses and brackets
Write simple expressions that record calculations described in words
Interpret and compare numerical expressions without evaluating them
Express a whole number from 2 to 50 as a product of its prime factors
Generate two numerical patterns from rules and relate their corresponding terms

ai.guidelines: Focus on evaluating numerical expressions with parentheses and brackets (order of operations), writing simple expressions from verbal descriptions (like "add 8 and 7, then multiply by 2" as 2 × (8 + 7)), comparing expressions without evaluating them, expressing whole numbers from 2 to 50 as products of prime factors, and generating two numerical patterns from rules to compare corresponding terms. Keep all values whole numbers and results within 0-1000.

topics.name: Base Ten 5th
topics.description: Decimal place value, powers of ten, and operations with multi-digit numbers and decimals
objectives: Understand that a digit is worth 10 times the place to its right and 1/10 the place to its left
Multiply and divide numbers by powers of 10
Read, write, and compare decimals to thousandths
Round decimals to any place
Fluently multiply multi-digit whole numbers
Divide whole numbers with up to four-digit dividends and two-digit divisors
Add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals to hundredths

ai.guidelines: Focus on decimal place value to thousandths (a digit is worth 10 times the place to its right), multiplying and dividing by powers of 10, comparing decimals with >, <, and =, rounding decimals to any place, fluent multi-digit whole-number multiplication, division with two-digit divisors and up to four-digit dividends, and adding, subtracting, multiplying, and dividing decimals to hundredths. Prefer clean values that work out exactly.

topics.name: Fractions 5th
topics.description: Adding and subtracting unlike denominators, and multiplying and dividing fractions
objectives: Add and subtract fractions with unlike denominators using equivalent fractions
Add and subtract mixed numbers with unlike denominators
Interpret a fraction as division of the numerator by the denominator
Multiply fractions by whole numbers and by other fractions
Predict how multiplying by a fraction changes a number without calculating
Divide unit fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by unit fractions

ai.guidelines: Focus on adding and subtracting fractions with unlike denominators (including mixed numbers) by finding equivalent fractions, interpreting a fraction as division of the numerator by the denominator, multiplying a fraction by a whole number or another fraction, multiplication as scaling (predicting whether a product is larger or smaller than a factor without calculating), and dividing unit fractions by whole numbers or whole numbers by unit fractions. Use denominators up to 12 and give answers in simplest form.

topics.name: Measurement & Data 5th
topics.description: Unit conversions, line plots with fractions, and volume of rectangular prisms
objectives: Convert among units within the metric and customary measurement systems
Interpret line plots with measurements in fractions of a unit
Understand volume as counting unit cubes with no gaps or overlaps
Find volumes of right rectangular prisms with V = l × w × h
Find volumes of solid figures composed of two rectangular prisms

ai.guidelines: Focus on converting measurement units within one system (like centimeters to meters or feet to inches, including decimal metric conversions), interpreting line plots whose measurements are fractions of a unit (1/4, 1/2, 3/4), understanding volume as counting unit cubes, computing volumes of right rectangular prisms with V = l × w × h, and finding volumes of figures made of two non-overlapping rectangular prisms. Keep conversions exact and volumes whole numbers.

topics.name: Geometry 5th
topics.description: The coordinate plane and classifying two-dimensional figures in a hierarchy
objectives: Use ordered pairs to name and locate points in the first quadrant
Understand that the coordinates tell distances from the axes
Solve real-world problems using points on a coordinate grid
Classify two-dimensional figures by their properties
Understand that properties of a category apply to all its subcategories

ai.guidelines: Focus on the first quadrant of the coordinate plane: naming points with ordered pairs (x, y), understanding that the first coordinate is the distance along the x-axis and the second along the y-axis, finding horizontal or vertical distances between points that share a coordinate, and classifying two-dimensional figures in a hierarchy (every square is a rectangle and a rhombus, every rectangle is a parallelogram, a trapezoid has exactly one pair of parallel sides). Keep coordinates whole numbers from 0 to 12.
